breakdown: Of great influence, of great authority (mahā + sakkha).

mahācattārīsakasuttaṁ.meaning: the Mahācattārīsakasutta, the Discourse on the Great Forty
mahācattārīsakasuttaṁ.gram: proper noun, neuter (title of a sutta)

mahāmaṅgalasuttaṁ, khuddakanikāye, khudd.meaning: the Mahāmaṅgalasutta (Great Discourse on Blessings), in the Khuddakapāṭha of the Khuddakanikāya
breakdown: The Great Discourse on Blessings (mahā + maṅgala + sutta).
The Minor Collection of the Sutta Piṭaka.
The Khuddakapāṭha, the first book of that collection.

mahāparinibbānasutta, mahāvaggapāḷi, dīg.meaning: the Mahāparinibbānasutta (Great Discourse on the Final Passing Away), in the Mahāvagga of the Dīghanikāya
breakdown: The Great Discourse on the Buddha's final passing into nibbāna.
The Great Division of the Dīghanikāya.

majjapānā.gram: neuter noun, compound (here ablative: "from drinking intoxicants")
majjapānā.goenka: Abstaining from majjapāna, the consumption of liquor and intoxicants, is the fifth precept of sīla. Intoxicants cloud sati and sampajañña, the very faculties a meditator works to sharpen; without a sober, balanced mind neither samādhi nor paññā can be developed.

majjhe.gram: adjective/noun majjha in the locative singular, used adverbially

majjhekalyāṇaṁ.summary: From the stock praise of the Dhamma: beneficial in the beginning, in the middle and in the end.

majjhimā.summary: As in majjhimā paṭipadā, the middle path avoiding the two extremes.

breakdown: Root: to smear; makkha is the smearing over of another's good qualities — denigration, hypocrisy.
Me (accusative form of the 1st person pronoun).
Because, indeed. (The niggahīta becomes ñ before h in this junction.)

mammacchedakakāyavacīpayogasamuṭṭhāpikā.meaning: occasioned by violent effort of body and speech that cuts to the quick
mammacchedakakāyavacīpayogasamuṭṭhāpikā.summary: Figuratively "breaking the joints": mamma is a nerve centre, a vital spot of the body.
breakdown: Nerve centre, vital spot of the body.
Cutting, breaking; mammacchedaka is figuratively "cutting at the vitals", violent.

mamuddesiko.meaning: referring to me; designated with respect to me
mamuddesiko.gram: adjective, compound (mama/maṁ + uddesika; masculine nominative singular)
breakdown: Me, of me (the final vowel fuses with u-).

manasi.summary: Familiar from manasi karoti, "to fix in the mind, to attend".
manasi.gram: masculine noun mano (mano-group, from √man) in the rare locative singular manasi

manasā.gram: masculine noun mano (mano-group) in the instrumental singular

manasānupekkhitā.meaning: examined by the mind; mentally pondered and observed
manasānupekkhitā.summary: From the stock description of teachings well learnt: verbally repeated and examined by the mind.
manasānupekkhitā.gram: past participle of the compound verb manasānupekkhati (here plural form)
breakdown: Looked at, observed (past participle of pekkhati).

manokammaṁ.goenka: Of the three kinds of action — bodily, verbal and mental — the Buddha gives manokamma, mental action, the primary place: every deed of body or speech is preceded and driven by volition in the mind. This is why the tradition insists that kamma is volition, and why purification must begin at the level of the mind, observing and ceasing to generate unwholesome mental reactions.

manomayā.summary: From the opening verses of the Dhammapada: all phenomena are mind-made.

manopubbaṅgamā.meaning: preceded by mind; with mind as forerunner
manopubbaṅgamā.summary: The opening word of the Dhammapada: manopubbaṅgamā dhammā, "mind precedes all phenomena".
manopubbaṅgamā.goenka: The famous opening of the Dhammapada — manopubbaṅgamā dhammā, manoseṭṭhā manomayā — declares that mind precedes all phenomena, mind is their chief, they are mind-made. In this tradition the verse is taken as the charter of practice: since suffering and happiness both originate in the mind, it is by purifying the mind, through the direct observation of its interaction with body at the level of sensation, that one's whole life changes.

manoseṭṭhā.meaning: with mind as the highest; mind is their chief
manoseṭṭhā.summary: From the same Dhammapada verse: mind is the chief of all phenomena.

manussabhūtānaṁ.meaning: of those who have become human; of human beings

manussapaṭilābho.meaning: the attainment of human existence; gaining a human birth

manussattapaṭilābho.meaning: the acquisition of the human state; the attainment of life as a human being
breakdown: Abstract suffix: manussatta, the human state.
Mind (weakened to manu- in the compound).
Suffix: manuñña, what gladdens the mind, pleasing.
Pleasant, charming, pleasing to the mind.
Pleasant, agreeable, pleasing to the mind.

maraṇadhammomhi.meaning: I am subject to death; death is my nature
maraṇadhammomhi.summary: From the five subjects for frequent reflection: "I am of the nature to die".
maraṇadhammomhi.gram: sandhi fusion: compound adjective + verb (maraṇadhammo + amhi "I am")
breakdown: I am. (The final -o fuses with a-: dhammo + amhi → dhammomhi.)

maraṇadhammā.meaning: subject to death; having death as their nature
breakdown: Here as final member of a compound: "having the nature of, subject to".

maraṇampi.summary: As in the First Noble Truth formula: "death too is suffering" (maraṇampi dukkhaṁ).
maraṇampi.gram: neuter noun maraṇa (nominative/accusative singular) + enclitic particle pi
breakdown: Too, also. (maraṇaṁ + pi → maraṇampi by sandhi.)

mattaññutā.meaning: moderation; knowing the right measure (especially in eating)

maññatha.meaning: what do you think?; you (plural) believe, imagine
maññatha.gram: verb, 2nd person plural present of maññati (√man)

maññissanti.meaning: they will think; they will believe
maññissanti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural future of maññati

maṁ.gram: personal pronoun, 1st person accusative singular (of ahaṁ)
maṁ.note: uncertain: the lexicon's gloss line for this row is garbled (it runs together with a comment about "not this Dhamma... the noble path of Dhamma"); the meaning given is the standard pronoun sense.

maṅgalāni.meaning: blessings; things conducive to welfare and good fortune

maṅgalasutta.meaning: the Maṅgalasutta, the Discourse on Blessings
maṅgalasutta.gram: proper noun, neuter (title of a sutta)
maṅgalasutta.note: uncertain: the source spelling maṅgāla- (with long ā) appears to be a typographical variant of the standard Maṅgalasutta.

maṇatīti.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present of maṇati + quotation particle ti
breakdown: 3rd person singular present ending; the final ti is the quotation marker (maṇati + iti → maṇatīti).

maṇḍūkadevaputtavimānavatthu.meaning: the Maṇḍūkadevaputtavimānavatthu, the story of the celestial mansion of the frog deva-son
maṇḍūkadevaputtavimānavatthu.gram: proper noun, compound (title of a Vimānavatthu story)

breakdown: I. (The final -o fuses with a-: maṇḍūko + ahaṁ → maṇḍūkohaṁ.)
Root: to pair, to couple; methuna is coupling, sexual intercourse.
Root: to pair, to couple; methuna is coupling, sexual intercourse.
